title: Express 5 Resource Design, Middleware Order, Async Errors, and Cancellation · Contract Lens: Feed Ts Response Finality
summary: Routes mirror tables, middleware mutates unvalidated request objects, async failures fall through inconsistently, responses double-send, and disconnects do not cancel dependencies. Predict the reader decision, external-unknown boundary, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL transaction, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed ts response finality increment in layered Express 5 feed-reader API. Learners progress thro…
objectives: Track headers, streaming, terminal response, cleanup, and error behavior so every request has one bounded outcome.
Compose request close, timeout, shutdown, and application ownership and propagate cancellation without undoing committed background jobs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Track headers, streaming, terminal response, cleanup, and error behavior so every request has one bounded outcome.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Response Finality model · Theory Feed Ts Respons…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Checking res.headersSent eliminates every response race.
